title: leontodon parviflorus
intro: Leontodon parviflorus, also known as the small-flowered hawk's beard or dwarf hawkbit, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which can help to alleviate digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-viral effects. Leontodon parviflorus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
